title: SQL Exception on Login
description: Steps to reproduce: Open login page, enter credentials, click login. Expected behavior: User logs in successfully. Error: DB timeout at AuthController.java:42
root_cause_prediction: SQL timeout issue due to inefficient query or high database load.
suggested_fix: Optimize SQL queries, ensure proper indexing, and increase database resources if necessary.
severity_reasoning: The issue is related to a timeout during login, which is indicative of an SQL query problem that could affect core functionality and performance.

title: Checkout Button Unresponsive
description: Steps to reproduce: Add product to cart and click checkout. Expected behavior: Payment page should open. Actual behavior: Nothing happens when clicking checkout.
root_cause_prediction: Button unresponsiveness is likely due to JavaScript errors or incorrect AJAX calls.
suggested_fix: Check for any JavaScript errors and ensure that the checkout button's click event is properly handled in the frontend code.
severity_reasoning: The issue does not involve security, system outage, duplicate payments, or any severe performance issues. It is a minor UI problem that can be fixed with basic frontend debugging.

title: PDF Export Corrupted
description: Steps to reproduce: Generate monthly report and export as PDF. Expected behavior: Valid PDF should download. Actual behavior: Downloaded PDF cannot be opened.
root_cause_prediction: The PDF generation process might have an SQL exception or database timeout issue.
suggested_fix: Investigate and correct any SQL exceptions or ensure the database connection is stable. Validate the report generation logic to ensure it does not time out.
severity_reasoning: The issue involves a PDF export corruption, which can be caused by SQL exceptions or database timeouts that might result in invalid PDF generation. Since this affects a core functionality (reporting), it is categorized as Medium.

title: Application Crash After Multiple Failed Logins
description: Steps to reproduce: Enter incorrect password 10 times continuously. Expected behavior: Error message and account lockout. Actual behavior: Application crashes unexpectedly.
root_cause_prediction: Application crash due to continuous login attempts leading to unexpected system behavior.
suggested_fix: Implement rate limiting on login attempts and error handling for excessive failed logins.
severity_reasoning: The application is crashing unexpectedly after multiple failed login attempts, which indicates a critical system failure and must be classified as High severity to ensure immediate attention.

title: Duplicate Login Crash Report
description: Steps to reproduce: Enter wrong password repeatedly during login attempts. Expected behavior: User receives warning messages. Actual behavior: Login page crashes after several failed attempts.
root_cause_prediction: Login page crash due to excessive failed login attempts causing a system-level timeout or resource exhaustion.
suggested_fix: Implement rate limiting for login attempts and add error handling to gracefully handle failed login scenarios without crashing the application.
severity_reasoning: The issue involves an application crash due to excessive login attempts, which is classified as High severity because it leads to a service outage and affects the core functionality of the login process.

title: User Profile Image Not Updating
description: Steps to reproduce: Upload a new profile picture and save changes. Expected behavior: Updated image should be displayed. Actual behavior: Old image continues to appear after refresh.
root_cause_prediction: The issue is likely due to a client-side rendering problem where the frontend does not correctly reflect the updated image.
suggested_fix: Ensure that the image update logic in the frontend correctly handles server responses and updates the displayed image accordingly. Verify that any caching issues are addressed if applicable.
severity_reasoning: The issue is related to a feature (user profile image update) that should be working correctly, but there are indications of a UI/UX problem where the user sees an outdated image. This suggests it's not a high severity issue like security or performance degradation.

title: Payment Processed Twice
description: Steps to reproduce: Double-click the Pay Now button during checkout. Expected behavior: Single payment transaction. Actual behavior: Customer account charged twice.
root_cause_prediction: The application's payment processing is being triggered multiple times by an unintended action.
suggested_fix: Implement a check to prevent double payments during checkout by ensuring that the 'Pay Now' button can be clicked only once per transaction. Review and possibly limit simultaneous requests in JavaScript or server-side code to ensure no duplicate transactions occur.
severity_reasoning: The bug involves a duplicate payment, which is indicative of a security or performance issue. The severity is Critical because it can lead to data corruption and customer dissatisfaction if not handled properly.

title: Password Reset Email Not Received
description: Steps to reproduce: Click Forgot Password and submit registered email. Expected behavior: Reset email should arrive within a few minutes. Actual behavior: No email received after 30 minutes.
root_cause_prediction: The password reset email not being sent could be due to a typo in the email address or incorrect configuration of the email service.
suggested_fix: Verify that the email address is correct and check if the email service (e.g., SMTP) is configured properly. If using an external service, ensure it's sending emails successfully. Check server logs for more details.
severity_reasoning: The issue is a cosmetic problem related to email functionality, which can be resolved without affecting core system performance or security.

title: Dashboard Loads Extremely Slowly
description: Steps to reproduce: Login and navigate to dashboard containing more than 500 records. Expected behavior: Dashboard loads within 5 seconds. Actual behavior: Dashboard takes over 45 seconds to load.
root_cause_prediction: The dashboard is experiencing high latency due to a large number of records and potentially inefficient database queries.
suggested_fix: Optimize database queries to reduce response time, consider pagination for larger datasets, or upgrade server resources if necessary.
severity_reasoning: The issue involves a significant performance degradation (dashboard takes over 45 seconds to load) which falls under the 'High' severity category.

title: Unauthorized Access to Admin Page
description: Steps to reproduce: Modify browser URL from user dashboard to /admin. Expected behavior: Access denied message. Actual behavior: User can access admin controls without authorization.
root_cause_prediction: The bug allows unauthorized access to admin page, which is a severe security vulnerability.
suggested_fix: Implement proper authentication and authorization checks to prevent unauthorized access. Update the admin URL so it requires user login with specific permissions.
severity_reasoning: The bug involves unauthorized access to an admin page, which is a critical security issue as it can lead to data breaches and potential loss of sensitive information.
